improvement: Bind the deployable Cloudflare Pages package to the exact GitHub commit during readiness classification and require the served well-known manifest to match that commit during the existing deployment verification step.

source_changes.effect: The workflow-invoked readiness command now writes cloudflare-static/.well-known/mirror-cartographer-research.json using GITHUB_SHA and GITHUB_REPOSITORY before deployment.
The workflow-invoked served-surface verifier now also fetches and validates the well-known manifest and fails closed on commit, repository, surface, privacy, HTTP, or JSON mismatch.
Adds deterministic coverage that the exact workflow commit and repository are stamped into the deployable manifest without secret or private-user-data flags.

verification.route: isolated Node.js execution using byte-equivalent committed readiness and stamper logic
assertions: readiness output does not emit credential values
well-known manifest contains the exact 40-character source commit
well-known manifest contains the exact owner/name repository identity

claim_state.observed: The repository source now connects the existing workflow-invoked readiness and served-verification commands to the deployment manifest identity chain.
claim_state.inferred: A future successful workflow run will include the stamped file in the cloudflare-static directory deployed by Wrangler and will reject a served deployment whose manifest does not match GITHUB_SHA.
claim_state.unresolved: No resolving Cloudflare Pages deployment or provider-returned URL was available in this run.

privacy.boundary: Only public repository paths, commit identifiers, deterministic test outcomes, and non-sensitive reason classes are recorded.

rollback: Revert commits be1091334cbdf30c98cdeed58965b297aca68675, a93dde4905bd02ba34304c83f598966cd42100d3, and 26f9e1b116dece717e5a85388d4c6dec2ab849d2 in reverse order.
